# Deep Review Dashboard - Session Overview

Auto-generated summary of the current review session, regenerated after every iteration evaluation from JSONL state and strategy. Never edited manually.

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Provides a compact, human-readable summary of the current review session. Serves three roles:
1. **Observability**, quick status check during long autonomous review runs
2. **Recovery surface**, second recovery channel alongside JSONL after context compaction
3. **Resume decision**, in confirm mode, shown to user before resume/restart choice

### Usage

- **Location:** `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-dashboard.md`
- **Generated by:** Orchestrator (step_generate_dashboard in YAML workflow)
- **Data sources:** `deep-review-state.jsonl` + `deep-review-strategy.md` + `deep-review-findings-registry.json`
- **Protection:** `auto-generated`, overwritten each iteration, never manually edited

+# Deep Review Iteration 001
+
+## Focus
+
+Dimensions: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability.
+
+Target files:
+
+- `spec.md`
+- `plan.md`
+- `tasks.md`
+- `FIXTURE.md`
+- `src/slugify.js`
+
+## Findings
+
+### P0: Length cap can leave a trailing hyphen
+
+The spec requires the final slug not to start or end with a hyphen and also requires maximum-length truncation to keep the slug valid [SOURCE: spec.md:18] [SOURCE: spec.md:19]. The implementation removes edge hyphens before applying the cap [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:20] [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:21], then slices the already-cleaned string without a second cleanup [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:23] [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:25].
+
+A title whose normalized form has a hyphen at the length boundary can therefore return a slug ending in `-`, violating the no-edge-separator rule. Example shape: an input that normalizes to 60 `a` characters, then `-b`, with the default cap of 60, returns the 60-character prefix ending in `-`.
+
+Adversarial self-check: this is not a fixture false positive. `FIXTURE.md` says the imperfections are intentional and must not be fixed [SOURCE: FIXTURE.md:11] [SOURCE: FIXTURE.md:13], but the review target is specifically seeded so review runs find defects. The spec/code contradiction is active.
+
+## Dimension Results
+
+| Dimension | Verdict | Evidence |
+|-----------|---------|----------|
+| Correctness | FAIL | Active P0 in max-length and edge-separator interaction. |
+| Security | PASS | No external input boundary beyond local string normalization, no I/O, no dependencies. |
+| Traceability | FAIL | Checklist says maximum-length enforcement is complete [SOURCE: tasks.md:7], but implementation violates the spec. |
+| Maintainability | PASS | Code is readable and localized; no separate maintainability finding. |
+
+## JSONL Delta Summary
+
+- New P0: 1
+- New P1: 0
+- New P2: 0
+- `newFindingsRatio`: 1.0
+
+Review verdict: FAIL

+## Active Finding Registry
+
+### DR-001: Length cap can leave a trailing hyphen
+
+- Severity: P0
+- Category: correctness
+- Status: active
+- Evidence: `spec.md:18`, `spec.md:19`, `src/slugify.js:23`, `src/slugify.js:25`
+- Finding: the implementation strips edge hyphens before truncation, then slices without re-stripping. If truncation lands on a separator, the returned slug can end with `-`, violating the spec.
+
+## Remediation Workstreams
+
+- If remediating outside the frozen fixture, apply truncation before final edge-hyphen cleanup or re-run edge cleanup after truncation, then verify a boundary case where the cap cuts at a hyphen.
+
+## Spec Seed
+
+- Requirement: final slug must not start or end with a hyphen.
+- Requirement: maximum-length truncation must keep the slug valid.
+
+## Plan Seed
+
+- Add a max-length boundary test that normalizes to a hyphen at the cap boundary.
+- Preserve the no-dependency CommonJS utility shape.

+# Deep Review Iteration 001
+
+## Focus
+
+Dimensions: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability.
+
+Target files:
+
+- `spec.md`
+- `plan.md`
+- `tasks.md`
+- `FIXTURE.md`
+- `src/slugify.js`
+
+## Findings
+
+### P0: Length cap can leave a trailing hyphen
+
+The spec requires the final slug not to start or end with a hyphen and also requires maximum-length truncation to keep the slug valid [SOURCE: spec.md:18] [SOURCE: spec.md:19]. The implementation removes edge hyphens before applying the cap [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:20] [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:21], then slices the already-cleaned string without a second cleanup [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:23] [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:25].
+
+A title whose normalized form has a hyphen at the length boundary can therefore return a slug ending in `-`, violating the no-edge-separator rule. Example shape: an input that normalizes to 60 `a` characters, then `-b`, with the default cap of 60, returns the 60-character prefix ending in `-`.
+
+Adversarial self-check: this is not a fixture false positive. `FIXTURE.md` says the imperfections are intentional and must not be fixed [SOURCE: FIXTURE.md:11] [SOURCE: FIXTURE.md:13], but the review target is specifically seeded so review runs find defects. The spec/code contradiction is active.
+
+## Dimension Results
+
+| Dimension | Verdict | Evidence |
+|-----------|---------|----------|
+| Correctness | FAIL | Active P0 in max-length and edge-separator interaction. |
+| Security | PASS | No external input boundary beyond local string normalization, no I/O, no dependencies. |
+| Traceability | FAIL | Checklist says maximum-length enforcement is complete [SOURCE: tasks.md:7], but implementation violates the spec. |
+| Maintainability | PASS | Code is readable and localized; no separate maintainability finding. |
+
+## JSONL Delta Summary
+
+- New P0: 1
+- New P1: 0
+- New P2: 0
+- `newFindingsRatio`: 1.0
+
+Review verdict: FAIL

+
+- Severity: P0
+- Category: correctness
+- Status: active
+- Evidence: `spec.md:18`, `spec.md:19`, `src/slugify.js:23`, `src/slugify.js:25`
+- Finding: the implementation strips edge hyphens before truncation, then slices without re-stripping. If truncation lands on a separator, the returned slug can end with `-`, violating the spec.

+## Remediation Workstreams

+- If remediating outside the frozen fixture, apply truncation before final edge-hyphen cleanup or re-run edge cleanup after truncation, then verify a boundary case where the cap cuts at a hyphen.

+## Spec Seed

+- Requirement: final slug must not start or end with a hyphen.
+- Requirement: maximum-length truncation must keep the slug valid.

+- Add a max-length boundary test that normalizes to a hyphen at the cap boundary.
+- Preserve the no-dependency CommonJS utility shape.

+# Deep Review Iteration 001
+
+## Focus
+
+Dimensions: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability.
+
+Target files:
+
+- `spec.md`
+- `plan.md`
+- `tasks.md`
+- `FIXTURE.md`
+- `src/slugify.js`
+
+## Findings
+
+### P0: Length cap can leave a trailing hyphen
+
+The spec requires the final slug not to start or end with a hyphen and also requires maximum-length truncation to keep the slug valid [SOURCE: spec.md:18] [SOURCE: spec.md:19]. The implementation removes edge hyphens before applying the cap [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:20] [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:21], then slices the already-cleaned string without a second cleanup [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:23] [SOURCE: src/slugify.js:25].
+
+A title whose normalized form has a hyphen at the length boundary can therefore return a slug ending in `-`, violating the no-edge-separator rule. Example shape: an input that normalizes to 60 `a` characters, then `-b`, with the default cap of 60, returns the 60-character prefix ending in `-`.
+
+Adversarial self-check: this is not a fixture false positive. `FIXTURE.md` says the imperfections are intentional and must not be fixed [SOURCE: FIXTURE.md:11] [SOURCE: FIXTURE.md:13], but the review target is specifically seeded so review runs find defects. The spec/code contradiction is active.
+
+## Dimension Results
+
+| Dimension | Verdict | Evidence |
+|-----------|---------|----------|
+| Correctness | FAIL | Active P0 in max-length and edge-separator interaction. |
+| Security | PASS | No external input boundary beyond local string normalization, no I/O, no dependencies. |
+| Traceability | FAIL | Checklist says maximum-length enforcement is complete [SOURCE: tasks.md:7], but implementation violates the spec. |
+| Maintainability | PASS | Code is readable and localized; no separate maintainability finding. |
+
+## JSONL Delta Summary
+
+- New P0: 1
+- New P1: 0
+- New P2: 0
+- `newFindingsRatio`: 1.0
+
+Review verdict: FAIL
